MPC.name: Massively Parallel Computer
MPC.cpu: Intel Xeon Max 9480 (1.9 GHz), 2 sockets x 56 cores = 112 cores/node
MPC.local_disk: ~960 GB NVMe per node (480 GB x 2)
MPC.role: Primary compute — most jobs run here

GPU.name: GPU Server (BWGPU)
GPU.role: Small GPU server, mainly for postprocessing — most HBW2 work is CPU

notes: HBW2 is a CPU-first system: the 312-node MPC (and the 2-node large-memory LMC) carry the bulk of the workload. There are only 4 GPU nodes, intended for postprocessing.
Jobs are described in CPU terms: --nodes, --ntasks-per-node (MPI ranks), --cpus-per-task (threads), and --mem. Set OMP_NUM_THREADS=$SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK for threaded code.
--account <projectID> is required for job execution (project IDs look like RB999999 for RIKEN, or HP-derived for HPCI). Check core-time with 'listcpu -p <project>'.
Architecture is x86_64 Intel Xeon — use the Intel oneAPI compilers (module load intel) and Intel MKL (-qmkl) for best performance.
GPUs are requested with --gpus on the gpu partition (1 GPU => 28 CPU cores). They are optional and rarely needed.
Default elapsed time is 1 hour if --time is omitted. The default memory per core is 1 GiB (MPC), 30 GiB (LMC), 4 GiB (GPU).
/tmp_work is scratch space, auto-deleted when its files are older than one week.
From compute nodes, reach the internet via the front-end Squid proxy: export https_proxy=$SLURM_SUBMIT_HOST:3128

modules.intel: Intel oneAPI Base & HPC Toolkit (compilers, MKL, Intel MPI); loading 'intel' also loads intelmpi

compilers.intel_classic: ifort / icc / icpc; MPI: mpiifort / mpiicc / mpiicpc
compilers.intel_llvm: ifx / icx / icpx; MPI: mpiifx / mpiicx / mpiicpx
compilers.gpu: nvcc (CUDA Toolkit) on the GPU server

storage.data: /data/<projectID> — per-project data area, 1 TB+ (usage fee; apply via portal)
storage.tmp_work: /tmp_work — temporary area, auto-deleted when files are older than 1 week
